html:
Margareta Eriksdotter may refer to:

  • Margaret of Denmark (in Sweden, Margareta Eriksdotter), a Danish princess and Swedish queen.
  • Margareta Eriksdotter (of the Eric family) (1155–1209), a Swedish princess and Norwegian queen.
  • Margareta Eriksdotter (of the Vasa family) (died in 1536), sister of Gustav Vasa.
  • Margareta Eriksdotter (of Leijonhufvud) (1516–1551), the second wife of Gustav Vasa, and Queen of Sweden from 1536 to 1551.
  • Margareta Eriksdotter (of Krummedige) (1390-1451).

How certain are we about this information?

From the Vasa era onwards, individuals are well documented in archives. The uncertainty lies more in interpretation and how later generations perceived them – especially since those in power themselves played a role in shaping historical narratives.
tldr: The text explains that the name "Margareta Eriksdotter" has been used by several different women throughout history, including multiple queens and princesses.
lattlast:
Because many important women in Swedish history shared the same name, it can be difficult to know which person is being discussed without additional information. The text lists five different individuals, all named Margareta Eriksdotter, including queens from various periods.

From the 16th century onwards, there is extensive documentation in the archives about what life was like. However, it is often more challenging to determine exactly what these individuals thought or felt, as those in power frequently controlled how their stories were presented to posterity.

When reading about these women, it's important to check the year or title associated with them. This helps distinguish between the queen who lived in the 14th century and the one who lived in the 16th century.
